<!doctype html>
<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width,initial-scale=1" />
<title>Numerical analysis</title>
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.css">
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/katex.min.js"></script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.11/dist/contrib/auto-render.min.js"></script>
<script>
(function(){
  function renderAll(){
    if(typeof renderMathInElement!=='function'){ return setTimeout(renderAll,50); }
    renderMathInElement(document.body,{
      delimiters:[
        {left:'$$',right:'$$',display:true},
        {left:'$',right:'$',display:false},
        {left:'\\(',right:'\\)',display:false},
        {left:'\\[',right:'\\]',display:true}
      ],
      throwOnError:false,
      macros:{
        '\\Spec':'\\Spec',
        '\\Gal':'\\Gal',
        '\\Hom':'\\Hom',
        '\\tr':'\\tr',
        '\\ad':'\\ad',
        '\\ind':'\\ind'
      }
    });
  }
  if(document.readyState==='loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded',renderAll);
  else renderAll();
})();
</script>
<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);
    font-family:-apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont,"Segoe UI",Inter,Roboto,sans-serif;
    font-size:17px;line-height:1.65}
  a{color:var(--blue)}
  code{font-family:ui-monospace,SFMono-Regular,Menlo,Consolas,monospace;
    background:#0b0f16;padding:1px 6px;border-radius:4px;color:#f0c987;font-size:0.92em}
  main{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:4rem 1.5rem 8rem}
  h1{font-size:2.4rem;letter-spacing:-0.01em;margin:0 0 0.4rem}
  h2{font-size:1.6rem;margin:3.2rem 0 0.8rem;color:#fff;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding-bottom:.4rem}
  h3{font-size:1.15rem;margin:1.8rem 0 .6rem;color:var(--yellow)}
  p{margin:0.7rem 0}
  .sub{color:var(--mute);margin-top:0;margin-bottom:2rem}
  .widget{background:var(--panel);border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:10px;
    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}
  .row{display:flex;gap:.6rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:center;margin:.4rem 0}
  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .55rem;font-family:inherit;font-size:.95rem}
  input[type="range"]{accent-color:var(--yellow);flex:1;max-width:260px}
  button{background:var(--panel2);color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:6px;padding:.35rem .7rem;cursor:pointer;font:inherit}
  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}
  .readout{background:#0b0f16;border:1px solid var(--line);border-radius:6px;
    padding:.6rem .8rem;font-family:ui-monospace,monospace;font-size:.92rem;color:#cbd5e1;
    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }
  .hero{padding:3rem 0 1rem}
  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}
  table.plain th,table.plain td{padding:.35rem .5rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);text-align:left}
  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */
</style>

1 Floating-point 2 Rootfinding 3 Quadrature 4 Linear systems 5 Finite differences 6 Finite elements

Numerical analysis

From a real-number problem to a finite computation: floating-point arithmetic, rootfinding orders, quadrature error scaling, conditioning of linear systems, finite-difference stability for the heat and wave equations, and the Galerkin finite-element method on a mesh. The bridge from real analysis and PDEs to a working algorithm.

blocks.html:
1. Floating-point arithmetic

A computer cannot store $\pi$. It stores an approximation of finite length. The IEEE 754 double-precision format encodes a real $x$ as $$ x \;\approx\; (-1)^s \cdot (1.m_1 m_2 \cdots m_{52})_2 \cdot 2^{e-1023}, $$ with $1$ sign bit, $11$ exponent bits, and $52$ mantissa bits — $64$ bits in total. The relative gap between consecutive representable numbers near $1$ is the machine epsilon $\varepsilon_M = 2^{-52} \approx 2.22\times 10^{-16}$.

Every basic operation $\circ\in\{+,-,\times,/\}$ produces a rounded result satisfying $$ \mathrm{fl}(a\circ b) \;=\; (a\circ b)(1+\delta), \qquad |\delta|\le \varepsilon_M. $$ A long computation chains these tiny errors. The key observation: the same numerical answer can come from a stable algorithm on a sensitive problem, or an unstable algorithm on a benign one.

Conditioning vs. stability

  • Conditioning is a property of the problem. A condition number $\kappa$ measures how much $f(x)$ moves when $x$ is perturbed: $\kappa = \sup_{\delta x} \frac{\|f(x+\delta x)-f(x)\|/\|f(x)\|}{\|\delta x\|/\|x\|}$.
  • Stability is a property of the algorithm. Backward stable means the computed $\hat y$ is the exact answer for some perturbed input $x+\delta x$ with $\|\delta x\|\lesssim \varepsilon_M\|x\|$.

Forward error $\le \kappa \cdot \text{(backward error)}$. A backward-stable algorithm on an ill-conditioned problem still loses $\log_{10}\kappa$ digits, and that's not the algorithm's fault.

Catastrophic cancellation
subtract two nearby reals to see digits evaporate
10⁻⁶
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg=$("#fp-svg"), out=$("#fp-out"), slider=$("#fp-x"), pill=$("#fp-xval");
  const W=760, H=220;
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const k = +slider.value;
    const x = Math.pow(10, -k);
    pill.textContent = `x = 10⁻${k}`;
    // Compute (1+x) - 1 in float -- the "naive" way
    const a = 1 + x;
    const naive = a - 1;
    // Better: just return x (exact)
    const exact = x;
    // Relative error
    const relErr = exact === 0 ? 0 : Math.abs(naive - exact) / Math.abs(exact);
    const lostDigits = relErr === 0 ? 0 : Math.min(16, Math.max(0, -Math.log10(relErr)));
    const goodDigits = Math.max(0, 16 - (16 - lostDigits));
    // Visual: digit bar
    const bx=40, by=70, bw=W-80, bh=40;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:bx,y:by,width:bw,height:bh,fill:"var(--panel2)",stroke:"var(--line)"}));
    // good digits = digits actually correct
    const correct = Math.max(0, Math.min(16, -Math.log10(Math.max(relErr, 1e-17))));
    const goodW = bw * correct/16;
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:bx,y:by,width:goodW,height:bh,fill:"var(--green)",opacity:0.7}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:bx+goodW,y:by,width:bw-goodW,height:bh,fill:"var(--pink)",opacity:0.6}));
    // tick marks every 2 digits
    for(let i=0;i<=16;i+=2){
      const tx = bx + i*bw/16;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:tx,y1:by-4,x2:tx,y2:by+bh+4,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":0.7}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:tx,y:by-8,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = i; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    const lab1 = SVG("text",{x:bx,y:by+bh+30,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--green)"});
    lab1.textContent = `correct digits ≈ ${correct.toFixed(1)}`; svg.appendChild(lab1);
    const lab2 = SVG("text",{x:bx,y:by+bh+48,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--pink)"});
    lab2.textContent = `lost to cancellation ≈ ${(16-correct).toFixed(1)}`; svg.appendChild(lab2);
    const ttl = SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:30,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":14,"font-weight":600,fill:"var(--ink)"});
    ttl.textContent = "(1 + x) − 1   computed in IEEE double"; svg.appendChild(ttl);
    out.textContent =
      `x        = ${exact.toExponential(8)}\n` +
      `1 + x    = ${a.toString().padEnd(20).slice(0,20)}\n` +
      `(1+x)−1  = ${naive.toExponential(8)}    (should equal x)\n` +
      `relative error = ${relErr.toExponential(3)}`;
  }
  slider.addEventListener("input", render);
  render();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: Pushing $x$ smaller than $\varepsilon_M$ collapses $1+x$ back to $1$ in finite precision; the subtraction returns $0$ instead of $x$. This is catastrophic cancellation: the relevant information was destroyed in the addition before the subtraction had a chance to recover it. The cure is algorithmic: rewrite $\sqrt{x+1}-\sqrt{x}$ as $1/(\sqrt{x+1}+\sqrt{x})$, or use Kahan summation when adding many terms.

2. Rootfinding: bisection and Newton

Given $f\colon [a,b]\to\mathbb{R}$ continuous with $f(a)f(b)<0$, the intermediate value theorem promises a root. Bisection exploits this: at each step, evaluate $f$ at the midpoint $m=(a+b)/2$ and replace whichever endpoint shares its sign. The interval halves on each step, so after $n$ iterations the root is bracketed in a window of width $(b-a)/2^n$.

If $f$ is differentiable, Newton's method linearizes near the current guess: at $x_n$, replace $f$ by its tangent, find where it crosses zero, $$ x_{n+1} \;=\; x_n - \frac{f(x_n)}{f'(x_n)}. $$ Near a simple root $r$ (where $f(r)=0$, $f'(r)\ne 0$), Taylor's theorem gives $|x_{n+1}-r| \le C|x_n-r|^2$ — quadratic convergence. The number of correct digits roughly doubles each step.

Order of convergence

A sequence $x_n\to r$ has order $p$ with rate $C$ if $|x_{n+1}-r|\le C|x_n-r|^p$ for $n$ large. Examples:

MethodOrder $p$Cost / iterNeeds
Bisection$1$ (linear, rate $1/2$)1 eval of $f$sign change
Secant$(1+\sqrt 5)/2\approx 1.618$1 eval of $f$two prior iterates
Newton$2$ (quadratic)1 eval of $f$, $f'$$f'(r)\ne 0$
Halley$3$ (cubic)$f$, $f'$, $f''$$f'(r)\ne 0$

Newton's iteration on $f(x)=x^2 - 2$
click "step" to advance — watch the tangents converge to $\sqrt 2$
x₀ = 2.5
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg=$("#newton-svg"), out=$("#newton-out");
  const stepBtn=$("#newton-step"), resetBtn=$("#newton-reset");
  const slider=$("#newton-x0"), pill=$("#newton-x0val");
  const W=760, H=320, padL=50, padR=20, padT=20, padB=40;
  const xmin=-0.5, xmax=3.2, ymin=-2.5, ymax=8;
  function X(x){ return padL + (x-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR); }
  function Y(y){ return padT + (ymax-y)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB); }
  const f = x => x*x - 2;
  const fp = x => 2*x;
  let iterates = [];
  function reset(){
    iterates = [+slider.value];
    pill.textContent = `x₀ = ${(+slider.value).toFixed(2)}`;
    render();
  }
  function step(){
    const xn = iterates[iterates.length-1];
    if(Math.abs(fp(xn)) < 1e-14) return;
    const xn1 = xn - f(xn)/fp(xn);
    iterates.push(xn1);
    render();
  }
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:Y(0),x2:W-padR,y2:Y(0),stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:X(0),y1:padT,x2:X(0),y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1}));
    // f(x) curve
    const pts = [];
    for(let i=0;i<=200;i++){
      const x = xmin + i*(xmax-xmin)/200;
      pts.push([X(x), Y(f(x))]);
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:"M"+pts.map(p=>p.join(",")).join(" L"),fill:"none",stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":2}));
    // y=0 horizontal already drawn; mark sqrt(2)
    const sx = X(Math.SQRT2);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:sx,y1:padT,x2:sx,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--yellow)","stroke-width":1.5,"stroke-dasharray":"4 4"}));
    const slab = SVG("text",{x:sx+5,y:padT+14,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--yellow)"});
    slab.textContent = "√2"; svg.appendChild(slab);
    // tangents and iterates
    iterates.forEach((xn, i) => {
      const yn = f(xn);
      const slope = fp(xn);
      // tangent line crosses y=0 at xn - yn/slope = next iterate
      const xnext = Math.abs(slope) < 1e-14 ? xn : xn - yn/slope;
      // draw tangent as line from (xn, yn) to (xnext, 0)
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{
        x1:X(xn), y1:Y(yn), x2:X(xnext), y2:Y(0),
        stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":1.5,opacity:0.85
      }));
      // vertical drop from xnext on axis to f(xnext)
      if(i < iterates.length - 1){
        svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{
          x1:X(xnext),y1:Y(0),x2:X(xnext),y2:Y(f(xnext)),
          stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1,"stroke-dasharray":"3 3"
        }));
      }
      // dot at (xn, f(xn))
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:X(xn),cy:Y(yn),r:4,fill:"var(--pink)"}));
      // dot on axis at xn
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:X(xn),cy:Y(0),r:3.5,fill:"var(--yellow)"}));
      const lab = SVG("text",{x:X(xn),y:Y(0)+18,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--yellow)"});
      lab.textContent = `x${i}`; svg.appendChild(lab);
    });
    // axis labels
    for(let xt=0; xt<=3; xt++){
      const tx = X(xt);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:tx,y1:Y(0)-3,x2:tx,y2:Y(0)+3,stroke:"var(--mute)"}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:tx,y:Y(0)+H-padB-Y(0)+0,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    }
    // readout
    const lines = iterates.map((xn, i) => {
      const err = Math.abs(xn - Math.SQRT2);
      return `x${i.toString().padEnd(2)} = ${xn.toFixed(12).padEnd(16)}   |x${i}−√2| = ${err.toExponential(3)}`;
    });
    out.textContent = lines.join("\n");
  }
  stepBtn.addEventListener("click", step);
  resetBtn.addEventListener("click", reset);
  slider.addEventListener("input", reset);
  reset();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
The error after each Newton step squares the previous error. Starting from $x_0=2.5$ with $|e_0|\approx 1.09$, three steps reach machine precision; bisection on the same interval would need $\sim 50$ steps. The price is $f'$: when the derivative is unavailable or expensive, the secant method substitutes a finite difference and pays a slightly worse golden-ratio order $p\approx 1.618$.

Failure modes. Newton diverges if $x_0$ is too far from the root (the tangent overshoots), if $f'(r)=0$ (multiple root — order drops to linear), or if iterates fall into a cycle. Robust solvers combine bisection's safety with Newton's speed: Brent's method.

See also
  • Real analysis — Differentiation · the linearization that powers Newton

3. Quadrature: trapezoid, Simpson, Gauss

Quadrature means numerical integration: approximate $\int_a^b f(x)\,dx$ by a finite weighted sum $\sum_i w_i f(x_i)$. Three families dominate, distinguished by how cleverly they place the nodes $x_i$ and choose the weights $w_i$:

  • Newton–Cotes: equally spaced nodes, weights chosen to integrate polynomials. Trapezoid rule (degree $1$), Simpson's rule (degree $3$), and onward.
  • Gauss–Legendre: nodes are zeros of orthogonal polynomials, weights from the Christoffel function. With $n$ nodes, exact for polynomials of degree $\le 2n-1$.
  • Adaptive: refine where $f$ changes rapidly, leave smooth regions alone.

Composite trapezoid and Simpson

Subdivide $[a,b]$ into $n$ panels of width $h=(b-a)/n$. The composite trapezoid rule sums trapezoids: $$ T_n(f) \;=\; h\!\left[\tfrac12 f(x_0) + f(x_1)+\cdots+f(x_{n-1}) + \tfrac12 f(x_n)\right], \qquad \int_a^b f - T_n(f) = -\frac{(b-a)h^2}{12}f''(\xi). $$ The error is $O(h^2)$: doubling $n$ shrinks it by $4$. Simpson's rule fits a parabola through three consecutive nodes and integrates it; the resulting error is $O(h^4)$ — doubling $n$ shrinks it by $16$.

Quadrature error vs. number of panels
log–log plot — slope = order of accuracy
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg=$("#quad-svg"), out=$("#quad-out"), sel=$("#quad-fn");
  const W=760, H=360, padL=70, padR=30, padT=30, padB=60;
  // log-log axes: x = log10(N), y = log10(|err|)
  const xmin=0, xmax=Math.log10(2048), ymin=-16, ymax=0;
  function X(v){ return padL + (v-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR); }
  function Y(v){ return padT + (ymax-v)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB); }
  const fns = {
    poly: { f: x => x*x*x*x, exact: 1/5, label: "x^4" },
    exp:  { f: x => Math.exp(x), exact: Math.E - 1, label: "e^x" },
    sin:  { f: x => Math.sin(Math.PI*x), exact: 2/Math.PI, label: "sin(pi x)" }
  };
  function trapezoid(f, n){
    const h = 1/n;
    let s = 0.5*(f(0) + f(1));
    for(let i=1;i<n;i++) s += f(i*h);
    return s*h;
  }
  function simpson(f, n){
    if(n%2) n++;
    const h = 1/n;
    let s = f(0) + f(1);
    for(let i=1;i<n;i++) s += (i%2 ? 4 : 2) * f(i*h);
    return s*h/3;
  }
  // Gauss-Legendre nodes/weights for n=2..8 on [-1,1], rescaled to [0,1]
  const GL = {
    2: [[-0.5773502691896257, 1], [0.5773502691896257, 1]],
    4: [[-0.8611363115940526, 0.3478548451374538],[-0.3399810435848563, 0.6521451548625461],
        [0.3399810435848563, 0.6521451548625461],[0.8611363115940526, 0.3478548451374538]],
    8: [[-0.9602898564975363,0.1012285362903763],[-0.7966664774136267,0.2223810344533745],
        [-0.5255324099163290,0.3137066458778873],[-0.1834346424956498,0.3626837833783620],
        [0.1834346424956498,0.3626837833783620],[0.5255324099163290,0.3137066458778873],
        [0.7966664774136267,0.2223810344533745],[0.9602898564975363,0.1012285362903763]]
  };
  function gauss(f, n){
    const nw = GL[n] || GL[8];
    let s = 0;
    for(const [t, w] of nw){
      const x = 0.5*(t+1); // [-1,1] -> [0,1]
      s += w * f(x);
    }
    return 0.5 * s;
  }
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const F = fns[sel.value];
    const Ns = [2,4,8,16,32,64,128,256,512,1024];
    // axis box
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:padL,y:padT,width:W-padL-padR,height:H-padT-padB,
      fill:"none",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    // grid
    for(let lx=0; lx<=3.5; lx+=0.5){
      const xx = X(lx);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:xx,y1:padT,x2:xx,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":0.5}));
      const t=SVG("text",{x:xx,y:H-padB+18,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = `10^${lx.toFixed(1)}`; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    for(let ly=ymin; ly<=ymax; ly+=2){
      const yy = Y(ly);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:yy,x2:W-padR,y2:yy,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":0.5}));
      const t=SVG("text",{x:padL-8,y:yy+4,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = `10^${ly}`; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // axis labels
    const xl = SVG("text",{x:(padL+W-padR)/2,y:H-padB+45,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    xl.textContent = "number of panels N (log scale)"; svg.appendChild(xl);
    const yl = SVG("text",{x:18,y:(padT+H-padB)/2,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)",
      transform:`rotate(-90 18 ${(padT+H-padB)/2})`});
    yl.textContent = "|error| (log scale)"; svg.appendChild(yl);
    // compute and plot trapezoid & simpson
    function plot(name, fn, color){
      const pts = [];
      Ns.forEach(n => {
        const v = fn(F.f, n);
        const err = Math.max(1e-17, Math.abs(v - F.exact));
        pts.push([X(Math.log10(n)), Y(Math.log10(err))]);
      });
      svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:"M"+pts.map(p=>p.join(",")).join(" L"),
        fill:"none",stroke:color,"stroke-width":2}));
      pts.forEach(([px,py]) => svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px,cy:py,r:3,fill:color})));
      return pts;
    }
    plot("trap", trapezoid, "var(--blue)");
    plot("simp", simpson, "var(--pink)");
    // Gauss-Legendre as 3 points (n=2,4,8 nodes)
    const glPts = [];
    [2,4,8].forEach(n => {
      const v = gauss(F.f, n);
      const err = Math.max(1e-17, Math.abs(v - F.exact));
      glPts.push([X(Math.log10(n)), Y(Math.log10(err))]);
    });
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:"M"+glPts.map(p=>p.join(",")).join(" L"),
      fill:"none",stroke:"var(--green)","stroke-width":2,"stroke-dasharray":"5 4"}));
    glPts.forEach(([px,py]) => svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px,cy:py,r:4,fill:"var(--green)"})));
    // legend
    const lg = [
      ["trapezoid (slope −2)","var(--blue)"],
      ["Simpson (slope −4)","var(--pink)"],
      ["Gauss–Legendre","var(--green)"]
    ];
    lg.forEach((row, i) => {
      const ly = padT + 12 + i*18;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:W-180,y1:ly,x2:W-150,y2:ly,stroke:row[1],"stroke-width":2.5}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:W-145,y:ly+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--ink)"});
      t.textContent = row[0]; svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    // numerical readout
    const rows = Ns.slice(0,7).map(n => {
      const t = trapezoid(F.f, n), s = simpson(F.f, n);
      const eT = Math.abs(t - F.exact), eS = Math.abs(s - F.exact);
      return `N=${n.toString().padStart(4)}   trap err = ${eT.toExponential(2)}   simp err = ${eS.toExponential(2)}`;
    });
    out.textContent = rows.join("\n");
  }
  sel.addEventListener("change", render);
  render();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
Trapezoid lines have slope $-2$ on the log–log plot — confirming the $O(h^2) = O(N^{-2})$ rate. Simpson's slope is $-4$. Gauss–Legendre with $n$ nodes integrates polynomials of degree $\le 2n-1$ exactly, so on $x^4$ (degree $4$), $n=4$ Gauss nodes nail it to machine precision while trapezoid still has $\sim 10^{-5}$ error at $N=1024$. The Gaussian advantage is worth a thousand panels.

When trapezoid wins. For periodic integrands integrated over a full period, the composite trapezoid rule converges spectrally — faster than any polynomial rate. The Euler–Maclaurin formula explains why: the boundary terms vanish.

See also
  • Real analysis — Riemann integral · the limit these rules approximate

See also
  • Real analysis — Riemann integral · the limit these rules approximate

4. Linear systems: direct and iterative

Solving $Ax=b$ for $A\in\mathbb{R}^{n\times n}$ splits into two regimes: direct methods (factor $A$, then back-substitute) and iterative methods (build a sequence $x^{(k)}\to x$). Density and structure decide which is faster.

Direct factorizations

FactorizationFormCostWhen to use
LU$PA = LU$$\tfrac23 n^3$generic dense $A$
Cholesky$A = LL^\top$$\tfrac13 n^3$SPD $A$
QR$A = QR$$\tfrac43 n^3$least squares; rectangular $A$
SVD$A = U\Sigma V^\top$$O(n^3)$rank, regularization, conditioning

The condition number

The condition number of an invertible $A$ is $\kappa(A) = \|A\|\,\|A^{-1}\|$. For $\|\cdot\|_2$ this equals $\sigma_{\max}(A)/\sigma_{\min}(A)$, the ratio of largest to smallest singular values. Perturbation theory: $$ \frac{\|\delta x\|}{\|x\|} \;\le\; \kappa(A)\!\left(\frac{\|\delta A\|}{\|A\|} + \frac{\|\delta b\|}{\|b\|}\right). $$ Rule of thumb: solving $Ax=b$ in IEEE double loses $\log_{10}\kappa(A)$ digits.

Hilbert matrix conditioning
$H_{ij}=1/(i+j-1)$ — pathologically ill-conditioned
n = 6
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg=$("#cond-svg"), out=$("#cond-out"), slider=$("#cond-n"), pill=$("#cond-nval");
  const W=760, H=320, padL=80, padR=30, padT=30, padB=50;
  const xmin=2, xmax=14, ymin=0, ymax=20;
  function X(v){ return padL + (v-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR); }
  function Y(v){ return padT + (ymax-v)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB); }
  // Hilbert condition number grows like (sqrt(2)+1)^(4n)/sqrt(n) — log10 ≈ 1.5n
  // We use a closed-form approximation
  function hilbertLogKappa(n){
    // log10(cond_2(H_n)) is approximately given by Todd's formula. Use small-n known values.
    const known = [1, 19.28, 524, 15514, 476607, 1.495e7, 4.754e8, 1.526e10, 4.932e11, 1.602e13, 5.221e14, 1.704e16, 5.5e17, 1.78e19];
    // n from 1 to 14 (index n-1)
    return Math.log10(known[Math.min(n-1, known.length-1)]);
  }
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const n = +slider.value;
    pill.textContent = `n = ${n}`;
    // box
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:padL,y:padT,width:W-padL-padR,height:H-padT-padB,
      fill:"none",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    // grid: y axis 0..20, x axis 2..14
    for(let yt=0; yt<=20; yt+=4){
      const yy = Y(yt);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:yy,x2:W-padR,y2:yy,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":0.4}));
      const t=SVG("text",{x:padL-8,y:yy+4,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = `10^${yt}`; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    for(let xt=2; xt<=14; xt+=2){
      const xx = X(xt);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:xx,y1:padT,x2:xx,y2:H-padB,stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":0.4}));
      const t=SVG("text",{x:xx,y:H-padB+18,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = xt; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // machine epsilon line at y=16 (cond ~ 10^16 means total digit loss in double)
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:Y(16),x2:W-padR,y2:Y(16),
      stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":1.5,"stroke-dasharray":"6 4"}));
    const eml = SVG("text",{x:W-padR-4,y:Y(16)-4,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":11,fill:"var(--pink)"});
    eml.textContent = "1/ε_M ≈ 10¹⁶"; svg.appendChild(eml);
    // curve
    const pts = [];
    for(let k=2; k<=14; k++){
      pts.push([X(k), Y(hilbertLogKappa(k))]);
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:"M"+pts.map(p=>p.join(",")).join(" L"),
      fill:"none",stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":2}));
    pts.forEach(([px,py],i) => {
      const k = i+2;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px,cy:py,r: k===n ? 6 : 3,
        fill: k===n ? "var(--yellow)" : "var(--cyan)"}));
    });
    // labels
    const xl = SVG("text",{x:(padL+W-padR)/2,y:H-padB+38,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    xl.textContent = "matrix size n"; svg.appendChild(xl);
    const yl = SVG("text",{x:24,y:(padT+H-padB)/2,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)",
      transform:`rotate(-90 24 ${(padT+H-padB)/2})`});
    yl.textContent = "condition number κ(H_n)"; svg.appendChild(yl);
    // readout
    const k = hilbertLogKappa(n);
    const lostDigits = Math.min(16, k);
    out.textContent =
      `n = ${n}\n` +
      `κ(H_n) ≈ 10^${k.toFixed(2)}\n` +
      `digits lost in double precision ≈ ${lostDigits.toFixed(1)}  (out of 16)\n` +
      `${n >= 11 ? "→ at this size, the solution to H_n x = b is essentially noise in IEEE double." : "→ direct LU on this matrix is still useful."}`;
  }
  slider.addEventListener("input", render);

blocks.html:
Iterative methods

For large sparse $A$, factorizations destroy sparsity (fill-in) and become impractical. Iterative methods sidestep the issue. Jacobi: split $A=D+L+U$ (diagonal, strict-lower, strict-upper) and iterate $x^{(k+1)} = D^{-1}(b - (L+U)x^{(k)})$. Gauss–Seidel uses the freshly-computed entries: $x^{(k+1)} = (D+L)^{-1}(b - U x^{(k)})$. Both converge iff the spectral radius of the iteration matrix is $<1$.

Conjugate gradient on SPD systems is essentially optimal. After $k$ iterations, the error in the $A$-norm satisfies $$ \|x - x^{(k)}\|_A \;\le\; 2\!\left(\frac{\sqrt{\kappa}-1}{\sqrt{\kappa}+1}\right)^{\!k}\!\|x - x^{(0)}\|_A. $$ So $O(\sqrt\kappa)$ iterations buy a fixed-factor reduction — exponentially better than Jacobi's $O(\kappa)$. Preconditioning (multiply both sides by $M^{-1}\approx A^{-1}$) shrinks the effective $\kappa$ further.

See also

Used in

5. Finite differences and CFL stability

Replace derivatives by finite differences on a grid. Three workhorses for $f'(x)$:

$$ \underbrace{\frac{f(x+h)-f(x)}{h}}_{\text{forward, }O(h)} \quad \underbrace{\frac{f(x)-f(x-h)}{h}}_{\text{backward, }O(h)} \quad \underbrace{\frac{f(x+h)-f(x-h)}{2h}}_{\text{central, }O(h^2)}. $$

For $f''$, the standard 3-point stencil is $\bigl(f(x-h)-2f(x)+f(x+h)\bigr)/h^2 = f''(x) + O(h^2)$.

Discretizing the heat equation

Discretize $u_t = u_{xx}$ on a grid $x_j = j\Delta x$, $t^n = n\Delta t$. The simplest explicit scheme is forward time, central space (FTCS): $$ u_j^{n+1} \;=\; u_j^n + \lambda\bigl(u_{j+1}^n - 2u_j^n + u_{j-1}^n\bigr), \qquad \lambda = \frac{\Delta t}{(\Delta x)^2}. $$ Insert a Fourier mode $u_j^n = g^n e^{i\xi j\Delta x}$. The amplification factor is $g(\xi) = 1 - 2\lambda(1-\cos(\xi\Delta x)) = 1 - 4\lambda\sin^2(\xi\Delta x/2)$. Stability — $|g|\le 1$ for all $\xi$ — demands $\lambda\le \tfrac12$, i.e. $$ \boxed{\;\Delta t \;\le\; \tfrac12 (\Delta x)^2.\;} $$ Cross this bound and the highest-frequency Fourier mode amplifies geometrically; round-off blows up.

The CFL condition

For the wave equation $u_{tt} = c^2 u_{xx}$, the explicit centered scheme has stability requirement $$ \frac{c\,\Delta t}{\Delta x} \;\le\; 1 \qquad\text{(Courant–Friedrichs–Lewy).} $$ Geometrically: the numerical domain of dependence (the stencil's reach) must contain the analytic one (the cone with slope $1/c$). If a node depends on data the stencil can't see, the scheme is doomed.

FTCS heat scheme — stable vs. unstable
drag $\lambda=\Delta t/\Delta x^2$ across the threshold $1/2$
λ = 0.40
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  const svg=$("#cfl-svg"), out=$("#cfl-out"), slider=$("#cfl-lam"), pill=$("#cfl-lamval");
  const stepBtn=$("#cfl-step"), resetBtn=$("#cfl-reset");
  const W=760, H=280, padL=60, padR=20, padT=30, padB=50;
  const N = 51; // grid points 0..N-1, x = i/(N-1) ∈ [0,1]
  let u = new Array(N).fill(0);
  let n = 0;
  function reset(){
    u = new Array(N).fill(0);
    // initial: bump in middle
    for(let i=0; i<N; i++){
      const x = i/(N-1);
      u[i] = Math.exp(-100*(x-0.5)*(x-0.5));
    }
    u[0] = 0; u[N-1] = 0;
    n = 0;
    render();
  }
  function step(times=10){
    const lam = +slider.value;
    for(let t=0; t<times; t++){
      const u2 = u.slice();
      for(let i=1; i<N-1; i++){
        u2[i] = u[i] + lam*(u[i+1] - 2*u[i] + u[i-1]);
      }
      u = u2;
      n++;
    }
    render();
  }
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const lam = +slider.value;
    pill.textContent = `λ = ${lam.toFixed(2)}`;
    // box
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:padL,y:padT,width:W-padL-padR,height:H-padT-padB,
      fill:"none",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    // detect blow-up
    let umax = 0; for(const v of u) umax = Math.max(umax, Math.abs(v));
    const yScale = umax > 1.5 ? 1.5 : 1.05;
    const ymin = -yScale, ymax = yScale;
    function X(i){ return padL + i/(N-1) * (W-padL-padR); }
    function Y(v){ return padT + (ymax - v)/(ymax-ymin) * (H-padT-padB); }
    // zero line
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:Y(0),x2:W-padR,y2:Y(0),stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":0.6}));
    // curve
    const pts = u.map((v, i) => [X(i), Y(v)]);
    const safe = umax < 100;
    const color = lam > 0.5 ? "var(--pink)" : "var(--cyan)";
    if(safe){
      svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:"M"+pts.map(p=>p.join(",")).join(" L"),
        fill:"none",stroke:color,"stroke-width":2}));
      pts.forEach(([px,py]) => svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px,cy:py,r:1.8,fill:color})));
    }else{
      const t = SVG("text",{x:W/2,y:H/2,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":18,fill:"var(--pink)","font-weight":700});
      t.textContent = "BLOWUP — |u| exceeded 100 (unstable)"; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    // x label
    const xl = SVG("text",{x:(padL+W-padR)/2,y:H-padB+30,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":12,fill:"var(--mute)"});
    xl.textContent = "x ∈ [0,1]   —   step n = " + n; svg.appendChild(xl);
    // threshold note
    const thr = SVG("text",{x:W-padR-10,y:padT+18,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":12,
      fill: lam > 0.5 ? "var(--pink)" : "var(--green)","font-weight":600});
    thr.textContent = lam > 0.5 ? `λ > 1/2 — UNSTABLE` : `λ ≤ 1/2 — stable`;
    svg.appendChild(thr);
    out.textContent =
      `λ = Δt / Δx² = ${lam.toFixed(3)}   threshold = 0.500\n` +
      `step count: ${n}\n` +
      `max |u_j|: ${umax.toExponential(3)}\n` +
      (lam > 0.5
        ? "Above the CFL threshold the highest-frequency Fourier mode amplifies as |1 − 4λ|^n; nudge Δt below 0.5 Δx² and the scheme settles."
        : "Below threshold: the bump diffuses smoothly toward zero, as the heat equation prescribes.");
  }
  stepBtn.addEventListener("click", () => step(10));
  resetBtn.addEventListener("click", reset);
  slider.addEventListener("input", () => render());
  reset();
})();
</script>

blocks.html: Implicit schemes (backward Euler, Crank–Nicolson) are unconditionally stable: any $\Delta t$ is safe. The price is a tridiagonal solve at every step. For stiff problems where the explicit step is forced absurdly small, the trade is decisive.

6. Finite elements and the Galerkin method

Finite differences live on a regular grid; finite elements live on an unstructured mesh and integrate the weak form of the PDE. For the model problem $-\Delta u = f$ on $\Omega\subset\mathbb{R}^2$ with $u=0$ on $\partial\Omega$, multiply by a test function $v\in H^1_0$ and integrate by parts: $$ \int_\Omega \nabla u \cdot \nabla v \;=\; \int_\Omega f v \qquad \text{for every } v\in H^1_0(\Omega). $$ This is the weak formulation. A weak solution $u\in H^1_0$ exists and is unique by Lax–Milgram.

Discretization

Triangulate $\Omega$ into a mesh $\mathcal{T}_h$ of size $h$. For each interior node $i$, let $\varphi_i$ be the piecewise-linear "hat" function with $\varphi_i(x_j)=\delta_{ij}$. The space $V_h = \mathrm{span}\{\varphi_i\}\subset H^1_0$ is finite-dimensional, and we look for $u_h = \sum_i c_i\varphi_i\in V_h$ satisfying the weak form against every $\varphi_j\in V_h$: $$ \sum_i c_i \int_\Omega \nabla\varphi_i\cdot\nabla\varphi_j \;=\; \int_\Omega f\varphi_j \quad\Longleftrightarrow\quad K\mathbf{c}=\mathbf{b}. $$ The stiffness matrix $K_{ij} = \int\nabla\varphi_i\cdot\nabla\varphi_j$ is sparse (each hat sees only its neighbours) and SPD.

Mesh refinement and the hat basis
subdivide the mesh — watch the FEM solution converge
N = 6, h = 1/6
blocks.script:
<script>
(function(){
  // 1D model problem: -u'' = f on [0,1], u(0)=u(1)=0, with f(x) = pi^2 sin(pi x)
  // Exact solution: u(x) = sin(pi x)
  const svg=$("#fem-svg"), out=$("#fem-out"), slider=$("#fem-n"), pill=$("#fem-nval");
  const W=760, H=380, padL=60, padR=30, padT=30, padB=50;
  const xmin=0, xmax=1, ymin=-0.15, ymax=1.2;
  function X(v){ return padL + (v-xmin)/(xmax-xmin)*(W-padL-padR); }
  function Y(v){ return padT + (ymax-v)/(ymax-ymin)*(H-padT-padB); }
  function solve(N){
    // mesh: x_0=0, x_N=1, h = 1/N
    const h = 1/N;
    // unknowns at interior nodes 1..N-1 (size N-1)
    // stiffness: tridiagonal with 2/h on diag, -1/h on off-diag
    const m = N - 1;
    const a = new Array(m).fill(2/h);
    const b = new Array(m-1).fill(-1/h);
    // load: f_j ≈ ∫ f φ_j ≈ h * f(x_j) using midpoint approx (good enough for plot)
    const rhs = [];
    for(let j=1; j<=m; j++){
      const xj = j*h;
      // exact load integral with f = pi^2 sin(pi x): ∫ pi^2 sin(pi x) φ_j dx
      // approximate with simple trapezoid on each adjacent element
      // Use exact: 2*(1 - cos(pi h))/h * sin(pi xj) ... but easier to use midpoint
      rhs.push(Math.PI*Math.PI*Math.sin(Math.PI*xj) * h);
    }
    // Thomas algorithm
    const c = b.slice();
    const d = rhs.slice();
    const aa = a.slice();
    for(let i=1; i<m; i++){
      const w = c[i-1]/aa[i-1];
      aa[i] -= w*c[i-1];
      d[i] -= w*d[i-1];
    }
    const x = new Array(m);
    x[m-1] = d[m-1]/aa[m-1];
    for(let i=m-2; i>=0; i--){
      x[i] = (d[i] - c[i]*x[i+1])/aa[i];
    }
    // pad with boundaries
    return [0, ...x, 0];
  }
  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const N = +slider.value;
    const h = 1/N;
    pill.textContent = `N = ${N}, h = 1/${N}`;
    // axes box
    svg.appendChild(SVG("rect",{x:padL,y:padT,width:W-padL-padR,height:H-padT-padB,
      fill:"none",stroke:"var(--line)","stroke-width":1}));
    // x-axis
    svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:padL,y1:Y(0),x2:W-padR,y2:Y(0),stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":0.6}));
    // exact solution
    const exactPts = [];
    for(let i=0;i<=200;i++){
      const x = i/200;
      exactPts.push([X(x), Y(Math.sin(Math.PI*x))]);
    }
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:"M"+exactPts.map(p=>p.join(",")).join(" L"),
      fill:"none",stroke:"var(--mute)","stroke-width":1.5,"stroke-dasharray":"4 4"}));
    // FEM solution
    const u = solve(N);
    const femPts = u.map((v, i) => [X(i*h), Y(v)]);
    svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:"M"+femPts.map(p=>p.join(",")).join(" L"),
      fill:"none",stroke:"var(--cyan)","stroke-width":2.2}));
    femPts.forEach(([px,py]) => svg.appendChild(SVG("circle",{cx:px,cy:py,r:3.5,fill:"var(--yellow)",stroke:"#0b0f16","stroke-width":1})));
    // hat function illustration: highlight one
    if(N <= 16 && N >= 4){
      const j = Math.floor(N/2);
      const xj = j*h;
      const hatX = [xj-h, xj, xj+h];
      const hatY = [0, 0.25, 0]; // visual height
      const hatPts = hatX.map((xx, k) => [X(xx), Y(hatY[k])]);
      svg.appendChild(SVG("path",{d:"M"+hatPts.map(p=>p.join(",")).join(" L"),
        fill:"var(--pink)",opacity:0.18,stroke:"var(--pink)","stroke-width":1.5}));
      const hl = SVG("text",{x:X(xj),y:Y(0.30),"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--pink)"});
      hl.textContent = `φ_${j}`; svg.appendChild(hl);
    }
    // mesh ticks
    for(let i=0;i<=N;i++){
      const xi = i*h;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:X(xi),y1:Y(0)-4,x2:X(xi),y2:Y(0)+4,
        stroke:"var(--ink)","stroke-width":1.2}));
    }
    // x labels
    [0, 0.5, 1].forEach(xt => {
      const t = SVG("text",{x:X(xt),y:Y(0)+22,"text-anchor":"middle","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = xt; svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    // y labels
    [0, 0.5, 1].forEach(yt => {
      const t = SVG("text",{x:padL-8,y:Y(yt)+4,"text-anchor":"end","font-size":11,fill:"var(--mute)"});
      t.textContent = yt; svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    // legend
    const lg = [
      ["exact: sin(πx)","var(--mute)"],
      ["FEM u_h(x)","var(--cyan)"]
    ];
    lg.forEach((row, i) => {
      const ly = padT + 18 + i*18;
      svg.appendChild(SVG("line",{x1:W-200,y1:ly,x2:W-170,y2:ly,stroke:row[1],"stroke-width":2.5}));
      const t = SVG("text",{x:W-165,y:ly+4,"font-size":12,fill:"var(--ink)"});
      t.textContent = row[0]; svg.appendChild(t);
    });
    // L2 error estimate
    let err2 = 0;
    for(let k=0;k<200;k++){
      const x = (k+0.5)/200;
      // u_h via linear interp
      const j = Math.min(N-1, Math.floor(x*N));
      const xj = j*h;
      const t = (x - xj)/h;
      const uh = (1-t)*u[j] + t*u[j+1];
      const ue = Math.sin(Math.PI*x);
      err2 += (uh - ue)*(uh - ue) / 200;
    }
    const errL2 = Math.sqrt(err2);
    out.textContent =
      `mesh size h = ${h.toFixed(4)}\n` +
      `‖u − u_h‖_{L²} ≈ ${errL2.toExponential(3)}\n` +
      `predicted O(h²) ≈ ${(0.4*h*h).toExponential(3)}\n` +
      `as h halves, the L² error should drop by ~4 (Aubin–Nitsche).`;
  }
  slider.addEventListener("input", render);
  render();
})();
</script>

blocks.html:
A priori error estimates

Let $u\in H^2$ be the true weak solution and $u_h\in V_h$ the Galerkin approximation. Céa's lemma says $$ \|u - u_h\|_{H^1} \;\le\; \frac{M}{\alpha}\inf_{v_h\in V_h}\|u - v_h\|_{H^1}, $$ where $M,\alpha$ are the continuity and coercivity constants of the bilinear form. Combining with linear interpolation error gives $$ \|u - u_h\|_{H^1} \le C h\, \|u\|_{H^2}, \qquad \|u - u_h\|_{L^2} \le C h^2\,\|u\|_{H^2}. $$ The $L^2$ rate is one order better, by an Aubin–Nitsche duality argument. Higher-order ($P^k$) elements lift these to $h^k$ and $h^{k+1}$ — at the cost of a denser stiffness matrix.

See also

  • Functional analysis — Banach & Hilbert spaces · the $H^1_0$ home

Connections

Numerical analysis is where infinite-precision mathematics meets finite hardware. Open frontiers cluster around three questions: which problems admit provably stable algorithms (the field of randomized numerical linear algebra and probabilistic backward error analysis); how to design schemes for structure-preserving integration of geometric PDEs (symplectic integrators, mimetic discretization); and how to exploit modern hardware (sparse on accelerators, mixed-precision iterative refinement, ML-accelerated solvers).

  • Partial differential equations — Weak solutions — FEM is the computational realization of the Galerkin idea introduced there.
  • Functional analysis — Hilbert spaces — the inner-product geometry that makes CG converge in $O(\sqrt\kappa)$ steps and that justifies Galerkin orthogonality.
  • Harmonic analysis — von Neumann stability analysis is Fourier analysis on the discrete grid; spectral methods extend trapezoid's spectral accuracy to non-periodic settings.
  • Real analysis — Differentiation — every Newton-style method, every $O(h^p)$ truncation estimate, is a Taylor remainder argument.
  • Probability theory — Monte Carlo and quasi-Monte Carlo methods turn high-dimensional integrals from $O(h^{-d})$ deterministic cost into $O(N^{-1/2})$ stochastic error, escaping the curse of dimensionality.
